hypothesis: Combining a Donchian breakout filter with the Ichimoku cloud trend filter isolates true directional moves while the ATR expansion filter weeds out low‑volatility false breakouts; this reduces whipsaws on the volatile 5‑minute APTUSDC market and improves risk‑adjusted returns.

Ichimoku cloud provides clear support/resistance zones; a price breakout above the upper cloud (or below the lower cloud) signals a strong directional move. Adding a volume oscillator filter ensures the breakout is backed by increasing market participation, while an ATR threshold confirms sufficient volatility, reducing false breakouts in low‑volatility periods. This combination should improve win‑rate and consistency of breakout trades on a 5‑minute chart.

Combining a breakout of the Ichimoku cloud with a confirmed expansion of volatility (ATR above its recent average) and a matching volume surge filters out false breakouts and captures only strong directional moves, while the ATR‑based stop‑loss and profit target preserve a favorable risk‑reward profile.

Combining Ichimoku cloud breakouts with an ATR volatility filter and volume oscillator confirmation should reduce false breakouts by requiring price to move beyond the cloud only when volatility is expanding and buying/selling pressure is strong, thereby improving win rate and Sharpe while preserving the breakout edge.

Combining the Ichimoku cloud to filter trades in the prevailing trend with a price‑breakout measured by a dynamic ATR band and a volume oscillator confirmation should generate more frequent, high‑probability entries while suppressing false breakouts caused by low‑volume spikes.

Combining a breakout above/below the Ichimoku cloud with a minimum ATR volatility filter and volume‑oscillator confirmation aligns trades with strong directional moves while discarding low‑volatility false breakouts, thereby reducing the high loss‑rate observed in the previous version.
